# Nightly Research Report — 2026-05-22

**Generated by:** Claude Code (claude-sonnet-4-6)
**Stack context:** Next.js 16.x · React 19.2.6 · TypeScript 5.x · Tailwind 3 · @xyflow/react ^12.10.0 · FastAPI 0.136.1 · SQLModel 0.0.38 · Alembic 1.18.4 · Python 3.12 · PostgreSQL 17 · Redis 7.4.6 (pinned)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

All application-layer packages remain on current stable releases; no new major framework versions require immediate action today. The most important new signal is a **13-CVE batch released for Next.js in May 2026**, patched in Next.js 16.2.6 (the current pin) — confirm the project lock file resolves to exactly this version. A separate **PyJWT information-disclosure CVE (CVE-2026-32597)** is relevant given the recent FEAT-113 migration to `joserfc`; joserfc is unaffected but the advisory is worth monitoring. Redis server **must be upgraded from 7.4.6 to 7.4.9** to patch five 2026 CVEs (CVSS up to 7.7) identified in yesterday's research — FEAT-112 verified the candidate tag; an implementation issue should now be created to apply the pin change.

On the product side, three spec-ready features remain unimplemented: **Narrative Summary Generation** (backend + frontend), **Low Trade Count Warning** (frontend only), and **Multi-Period Batch Backtesting** (frontend + backend). All three are low-to-medium risk and have no blocking dependencies. The UX research confirms AI-assisted strategy building via natural-language-to-blocks is the sharpest competitive differentiator available to Blockbuilders in 2026 — worth flagging for the product roadmap.

---

## 2. Findings for Active Issues

### React / Next.js (Frontend)

**React 19.2.6** — current stable. A critical unauthenticated RCE in React Server Components was patched in December 2025; the project's current version is post-patch. No new action required.

**Next.js 16.2.6** — the May 2026 security batch patched **13 vulnerabilities** in this exact version. Key CVEs:

| CVE | Severity | Description |
|---|---|---|
| CVE-2026-44578 | High | WebSocket SSRF — unauthenticated access to internal resources via WebSocket upgrade |
| CVE-2026-23869 | CVSS 7.5 | DoS in React Server Components |
| Additional CVEs | Medium–High | Middleware bypass, XSS, cache poisoning, DoS |

**Action:** Confirm `frontend/package-lock.json` resolves `next` to `16.2.6`. Versions 13.x and 14.x receive no patches and should never be used.

**TypeScript** — TypeScript 6.0.3 is the current stable release (April 2026). The project is on 5.x (`^5.9.0` per FEAT-117). TypeScript 7.0 Beta (Go-based rewrite, ~10× faster) was released April 21, 2026; it is not production-ready. No action required this cycle; plan a TypeScript 6.x upgrade as a medium-priority maintenance task.

**Tailwind CSS 4.3.0** — released May 8, 2026. The project currently pins Tailwind v3 (FEAT-117 explicitly preserved v3 config). No migration needed now; the v3→v4 migration is a substantial breaking change (new config format, CSS-first config). Track but do not attempt without a dedicated spec.

### XyFlow / Canvas (FEAT-109, FEAT-110, FEAT-111)

**@xyflow/react 12.10.2** — current stable, project is pinned to `^12.10.0`. No breaking API changes since yesterday's report. Key capabilities confirmed stable:

- `useNodesData` + `useHandleConnections` hooks for reactive flows (relevant to FEAT-109 inline badges)
- `colorMode` prop (`light`/`dark`/`system`) — free dark mode for trading dashboards
- Server-Side Rendering support — useful for shareable result links (product.md §5.5)
- ELK layout engine integration supported — relevant to FEAT-016 (Auto-Layout & Connection Tidying)

The **two-click port connection pattern** (click output → click input) remains industry standard (used by n8n, Make.com). Badge tap targets on canvas nodes must use `e.stopPropagation()` to avoid triggering connection drag.

### FastAPI / Backend

**FastAPI 0.136.1** — project is already on this version (FEAT-116 completed). Confirmed stable.

**Breaking change note (v0.132+):** Content-Type header validation on JSON requests is now enforced. Verify all test HTTP clients send `Content-Type: application/json` on POST/PATCH requests with JSON bodies; any raw `httpx` calls without explicit headers may fail.

**SQLModel 0.0.38** — current stable (April 2, 2026). Python 3.9 support dropped; project uses 3.12. `sqlmodel-slim` discontinued — use `sqlmodel` directly (already the case).

### Redis

**Current pin:** `redis:7.4.6-alpine` (set in FEAT-107, verified in FEAT-112).
**Target pin:** `redis:7.4.9-alpine` — patches all five 2026 CVEs.

| CVE | CVSS | Description |
|---|---|---|
| CVE-2026-23479 | 7.7 | Use-After-Free in unblock client flow |
| CVE-2026-25243 | 7.7 | Invalid memory access in RESTORE command |
| CVE-2026-25588 | 7.7 | Invalid memory access in RESTORE + RedisTimeSeries |
| CVE-2026-25589 | 7.7 | Invalid memory access in RESTORE + RedisBloom |
| CVE-2026-23631 | 6.1 | Lua Use-After-Free |

All require authenticated Redis access; internal deployment reduces exposure, but patching is still required. **Action:** Create an implementation issue to update both `docker-compose.yml` and `docker-compose.prod.yml` from `redis:7.4.6-alpine` → `redis:7.4.9-alpine`.

**RQ 2.9.0+** — significantly ahead of the v1.x series. Notable additions: `SpawnWorker` (multiprocessing.spawn for Windows), Valkey support, configurable job callbacks. Check the project's `rq` pin in `backend/requirements.txt` and consider upgrading if still on 1.x.

## 3. Security Risks

### Critical / High (immediate attention)

| Item | Severity | Status | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Next.js 13-CVE batch (May 2026) | High | Patched in 16.2.6 | Verify lockfile resolves to 16.2.6 |
| Redis 2026 CVEs (5 CVEs, CVSS up to 7.7) | High | 7.4.9 available | Create issue to bump image pin |
| CVE-2026-44578 Next.js WebSocket SSRF | High | Patched in 16.2.6 | Confirm no custom WebSocket upgrade paths |

### Medium (track and plan)

| Item | Severity | Status | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| PyJWT CVE-2026-32597 (crit header bypass) | High | Patched in PyJWT 2.12.0 | Project migrated to `joserfc` (FEAT-113) — unaffected, but audit joserfc for same RFC 7515 compliance |
| python-jose CVE-2025-61152 (alg=none bypass) | Critical | `python-jose` removed (FEAT-113) | No action — already mitigated |
| Starlette CVE-2025-62727 (DoS) | High | Starlette resolved to 0.52.1 (FEAT-105) | No action — already mitigated |
| CVE-2024-47874 Starlette multipart DoS | CVSS 8.7 | Fixed in 0.40.0, project on 0.52.1 | No action — already mitigated |
| TypeScript 5.x → 6.x migration | Low | Deferred | Plan upgrade; v6 sets `strict: true` default |

### Clean / No Action

- **Alembic 1.18.4** — no known CVEs
- **bcrypt** — no new CVEs; 72-byte truncation is a known design limit, not a vulnerability
- **Stripe SDK** — no security patches; Python SDK updated to API version 2026-04-22

## 4. Implementation References

### Spec-Ready Features (no issue assigned)

#### Narrative Summary Generation (Backend + Frontend)

Spec in `docs/product/product.md §5.4`. Backend: `backend/app/backtest/narrative.py` (deterministic template rendering from existing metrics; ≤200ms overhead target). Frontend: narrative card as first element on results page, zero-trade path hides all metrics and shows "Modify Strategy" CTA, fires `narrative_viewed` PostHog event on viewport entry.

**Risk:** Low. No new endpoints, no schema changes. Uses existing `backtest_runs` fields.

**Reference implementation pattern:** deterministic template engine using f-strings over the existing summary metrics dict. No AI inference required.

#### Low Trade Count Warning (Frontend Only)

Spec in `docs/product/product.md §5.4`. Render yellow banner when `num_trades` is 1–9. Exact copy specified. Fire `health_warning_shown` with `warning_type: low_trade_count`. WCAG 2.1 AA contrast required.

**Risk:** Very low. Frontend-only, single component, uses existing `num_trades` field.

#### Multi-Period Batch Backtesting

Spec in `docs/product/product.md §5.1`. Preset period checkboxes (`30d`, `60d`, `90d`, `120d`, `1y`; Premium adds `2y`/`3y`). "Run All" enqueues one job per period. Results fill progressively grouped by period.

**Risk:** Medium. Requires backend changes (batch enqueue endpoint or extended `POST /backtests`), frontend polling/progressive update, and plan-tier gating for `2y`/`3y`.

### AI-Assisted Strategy Building (Competitive Differentiator)

Research confirms that **natural language → blocks** is the sharpest competitive differentiator available in 2026. Pattern used by emerging platforms:

1. User describes strategy in plain English: *"Buy when RSI < 30, sell at 5% profit"*
2. Backend parses intent → generates block definition JSON (same format as `definition_json`)
3. Canvas renders the generated strategy; user can edit freely
4. Optional iterative refinement: *"Add a stop loss at 3%"* appends blocks without full rebuild

**Implementation path for Blockbuilders:**
- The existing `definition_json` format is already structured for machine generation
- A Claude API call with the `definition_json` schema as a tool parameter could generate valid strategies from natural language input
- The Strategy Explanation Generator (product.md §4.8) is the reverse operation — already specced, proving the schema is AI-legible
- Risk: **high** (new AI dependency, billing integration, abuse vectors) — requires explicit human approval before implementation

### Canvas Auto-Layout (FEAT-016)

ELK layout engine (Eclipse Layout Kernel, available as `elkjs` on npm) is the recommended approach for auto-arranging node-based flows. It handles both left→right and top→bottom layouts with minimal wire crossings. `elkjs` is a pure-JS port with no native dependencies — safe to add. Estimated bundle size: ~650 KB gzip (lazy-loadable on "Auto-arrange" click).

Alternative: dagre (lighter, ~100 KB) but less actively maintained. ELK preferred for complex graphs.

---

## 5. Questions for the Team

1. **Redis image bump:** FEAT-112 confirmed `redis:7.4.9-alpine` is available and patches all 2026 CVEs. Should this be implemented as a standalone low-risk issue this week, or bundled with another infra task?

2. **TypeScript 6.x upgrade:** The project is on `^5.9.0`. TypeScript 6.0 sets `strict: true` as the default and changes module/target defaults. Should this be scoped as a maintenance feature (FEAT-118)? It is a medium-effort migration given the strict-mode default change.

3. **Tailwind v4 migration:** The project intentionally stays on Tailwind v3 (FEAT-117). Tailwind v4 has been stable since early 2026. When should the migration be scoped? It requires a new config format and removes several v3 patterns. Recommend deferring to a dedicated sprint.

4. **Narrative Summary feature:** This is the highest-value unimplemented spec-ready feature. Can it be assigned to the next Codex Web cycle? It unblocks the Low Trade Count Warning (which depends on the narrative card placement rules).

5. **Natural language strategy input:** Is the team ready to evaluate an AI-assisted strategy building proof-of-concept? The existing definition JSON format makes this technically feasible. It would require a Claude API integration, cost/rate-limit policy, and a dedicated risk assessment.
